title: × Dactylocamptis schulzii
intro:
There is no information available on the plant '× Dactylocamptis schulzii'. However, I can provide information on the orchid genus Dactylorhiza, as × Dactylocamptis schulzii is possibly an obsolete or misnamed hybrid of Dactylorhiza.

Dactylorhiza is a genus of orchids, commonly known as marsh orchids or bog orchids. They are terrestrial, herbaceous plants with underground storage organs and flowers that vary in color and shape.

The health benefits of Dactylorhiza include its potential to help manage blood sugar levels, lower cholesterol, and act as a natural diuretic. However, more research is needed to confirm these benefits.

The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. These compounds have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

Dactylorhiza is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures. The most common preparations include Dactylorhiza maculata, also known as spotted marsh orchid, which has been used in traditional medicine for its potential to help with respiratory issues and as a natural expectorant.
